signals.headline: SEC Drops Long-Running Lawsuit Against Ripple Labs | YES | HIGH confidence** [1][2]
signals.raw:
SEC Drops Long-Running Lawsuit Against Ripple Labs | YES | HIGH confidence** [1][2]

The SEC's decision to drop its lawsuit against Ripple provides definitive regulatory clarity, resolving a multi-year overhang that suppressed XRP's price and enabling institutional adoption.[1][2] This confirmation, alongside the full-scale rollout of Ripple's RLUSD stablecoin on XRP Ledger and Ethereum, has drive

signals.headline: **IMF Article IV Consultation (April 2, 2026)**: Projects U
signals.raw: **IMF Article IV Consultation (April 2, 2026)**: Projects U.S. GDP at 2.4% for 2026, core PCE inflation at 2.6% by year-end (missing 2% target until 2027), and minimal Fed rate cuts (fed funds from 3.6% to 3.4%), citing little room for easing absent labor/inflation downturns; Powell endorsed "soft landing."[1]
matched_markets: Will Powell say "Tariff inflation" during April press conference?

signals.headline: **FOMC March 18 statement**: Held rates at 3
signals.raw: **FOMC March 18 statement**: Held rates at 3.5-3.75%, noting solid GDP growth, low job gains, somewhat elevated inflation, and Middle East uncertainties; committed to 2% target.[5]

signals.headline: **S&P Global April outlook**: Cut 2026 global GDP to 2
signals.raw:
**S&P Global April outlook**: Cut 2026 global GDP to 2.4% on higher inflation/energy prices squeezing incomes; U.S. specifics align with broader downside.[4]

These are not "right now" (all pre-April 22), with next catalysts like April 28-29 FOMC or March CPI (due ~April 11, per [3]). No April GDP data yet (Q1 typically late April).[1][2][3][4][5]

signals.headline: **March FOMC minutes (released April 8)**: More officials see possible 2026 rate hikes due to oil/gas price spikes from
signals.raw: **March FOMC minutes (released April 8)**: More officials see possible 2026 rate hikes due to oil/gas price spikes from Iran conflict; markets now expect no cuts until late 2027; Powell tied cuts to steady inflation cooling.[3]
